metadata.preview_text: Your 3+3 bundle has been working hard for 75 days. Here's what to ask yourself, and when to reorder.
metadata.preheader_strategy: Extends subject with curiosity + utility, no false urgency
metadata.email_goal: Gentle replenishment reminder + upsell to 5+5 for appropriate segments
metadata.cta_priority: Education-first: remind of usage → suggest reorder → low-pressure CTA
metadata.sender_identity: Lena (Customer Support · Mom of two autistic sons)

copy.preview:
> Your 3+3 bundle has been working hard for 75 days. Here's what to ask yourself, and when to reorder.

- **Character count:** 98 (under 100 ✓)
- **Reasoning:** Extends the subject by naming the specific timeline (75 days) and sets expectation that this informative, not pushy. "Ask yourself" frames agency with the reader.

sections.name: SECTION 1, The "Whether...Or...If..." Opener (Observation + Recognition Anchors)
sections.content:
Whether your child is showing first signals or still in the familiarization phase, whether you're seeing mini-wins each week or wondering if it's doing anything at all, if your 3+3 bundle has been in the rotation for 75 days, you've already done the hardest part: consistent exposure to the Body-Signal Learning Layer.

And that consistency is the variable that matters most.

*(R1: symptom description over level labels ✓ | R3: age-agnostic ✓ | R5: timeline flexibility ✓ | Whether...Or...If... pattern ✓)*

---

### SECTION 2, Recognition Anchors Block (R2: 3-5 anchors)

At 75 days, parents tell me they're usually in one of these places:

- He's starting to pause before an accident. Not every time. But sometimes. And that's new.
- She tolerates the underwear now, doesn't fight it, doesn't ask to take it off. That's sensory progress you didn't have 10 weeks ago.
- Nothing obvious has changed yet. And you're wondering if this still on track.
- You've seen real progress, dry stretches, awareness moments, even a few successful potty trips. And now you're wondering: what's next?
- You took a break somewhere in those 75 days. Maybe a week. Maybe a month. That's normal here.

Wherever you are on that list, every single one of those is a real outcome. And every single one tells me the underwear is doing its job: giving the nervous system information it was missing before.

*(R2: 5 recognition anchors spanning L1-L3 ✓ | R4: verbal "telling me" + non-verbal "pausing" paired ✓ | R6: low-bar mini-wins throughout ✓)*

---

### SECTION 3, The Three-Family Testimonial Pattern (GF Inclusion)

Three families, three different timelines, one common thread:

> "He went from 'almost trained' to 'mostly there' around the 8-week mark. The rotation meant we always had a clean pair ready when he needed to go."
>, Sarah, mom of a verbal 4-year-old *(L1 anchor)*

> "She finally started pausing before accidents in Week 6. That pause, that split-second where you can see her brain registering 'something happened', is everything I was hoping for."
>, Lisa, mom of a 6-year-old with sensory differences *(L2 anchor)*

> "He's not trained. But he's feeling the wetness for the first time in 7 years. That alone was worth the 75 days. The rotation means the underwear is always part of his routine now, and every small signal builds on the last."
>, Maria, mom of a non-verbal 8-year-old *(L3 anchor)*

If any of those sound familiar, if you've seen even one small change in 75 days, the signal-wiring is happening. It just happens on different timelines for different nervous systems.

*(Three-Family Testimonial Pattern ✓ | L1/L2/L3 all represented ✓ | R4: verbal "said" + non-verbal sign examples ✓ | R6: success defined as mini-wins ✓)*

---

### SECTION 4, Validation + Mechanism Reframe

I want to be honest with you: 75 days into the Body-Signal approach, some families are seeing real momentum. Others are still in the "wait and watch" phase. Both are legitimate outcomes.

Here's what I've learned watching thousands of families go through this:

The underwear doesn't train your child. It *informs* their nervous system. The signal has to reach the brain, and for some kids, that pathway takes longer to wire. The 3+3 rotation means that signal is available every single day, multiple times a day, without you having to do anything extra.

If you've seen nothing yet: That's not a sign it's not working. It's a sign the wiring is still in progress. Some kids need 8, 12, even 16 weeks before the first observable signal.

If you've seen mini-wins: The pathway is building. Keep going. The next 75 days often look very different from the first 75.

*(R5: timeline flexibility with ranges ✓ | R6: success = mini-wins ✓ | V8 realistic ✓)*

---

### SECTION 5, The Rotation Value (Product Context)

The 3+3 bundle was designed for exactly this: 6 pairs in rotation so your child always has a clean, signal-ready pair available. At 75 days of consistent wear, the fabric is doing its job, but if you're doing laundry more than twice a week, you might benefit from expanding the rotation.

5+5 bundle adds 4 more pairs, enough for a full two-week cycle without scrambling. More pairs = more signal opportunities = less laundry stress for you.

*(Gentle upsell, not aggressive ✓ | V1(Warm) ✓ | No hard-sell language ✓)*

---

### SECTION 6, Low-Pressure CTA

If the 3+3 is still working for your family, great. No need to change anything. The underwear will keep doing its job for many more weeks.

If you're ready to expand the rotation, or if you just want to have fresh pairs on hand, here's the reorder link:

[Refresh Your 3+3 →]
[Explore the 5+5 Bundle →]

No rush. The signal-wiring happens on your child's timeline, not ours.

*(Permission-based CTA ✓ | No urgency/guilt ✓ | 60/40 hope/realism ✓)*

---

Next time: The final check-in — what 150 days of rotation means for the long game.

```
Talk soon,

Lena,
Customer Support · Mom of two autistic sons

P.S. If your child is still not showing any awareness signals after 75 days, that's
not failure. Some kids need longer. Reply if you want me to look at your specific
situation. I read everything.
```
